b98cbb1aa3d6edaf.name: list_storage_accounts
b98cbb1aa3d6edaf.reason: The query was never executed (no azure_graph credential), so no storage account names or resource groups were returned — only an unrun statement.
friction: resourcegraph_query is the only data-bearing object in the catalog and it hard-fails without an 'azure_graph' secret, but nothing in the browsable surface says how to attach one — no CREATE SECRET example, no required-fields list (tenant/client id/secret?). kql_recipes is advertised as the credential-free entry point, yet every recipe it offers is unusable until that undocumented setup step happens.

The failure is only discoverable by running a query. Neither list_tables nor describe_function's summary line flags the credential requirement up front; it appears mid-paragraph in doc_llm ('Requires an app-only azure_graph secret'), so an agent that reads only the description and argument docs will write a correct query and still hit a wall.

9b1acaa8bf93f7eb.name: scope_to_subscription

friction: The `subscriptions` argument description was truncated in describe_function output, so the exact separator convention had to be taken from the function-level doc_llm ('comma-separated GUIDs; empty = every readable subscription') rather than the per-argument doc.

No sample or test credential is available in the catalog, so no query against resourcegraph_query can be executed end-to-end — only the credential-free kql_recipes view is queryable. The error message is clear about what is missing but does not say how to create the azure_graph secret (no CREATE SECRET example in any doc string I could reach).
